statusLine: EU fuel supplies remain under strain with 14 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, while crude oil prices have surged 32% since February's Middle East escalation.
fullAnalysis:
Despite EU-wide averages appearing adequate with petrol at 113 days and diesel at 102.2 days above the 90-day mandatory minimum, the distribution reveals significant vulnerabilities. Romania faces the most severe shortages with all three fuel types in critical status, including petrol at just 40.4 days and diesel at 30.2 days. Poland also shows comprehensive strain with petrol at 66.6 days and diesel at 74.7 days, both well below emergency thresholds.

Jet fuel presents the most widespread concern, with 17 of 27 countries in critical or warning status and an EU average of just 96.4 days. This reflects heightened aviation fuel demand and supply chain pressures. Major economies including Germany, France, Italy, and Spain all show critical jet fuel reserves below 68 days. The concentration of shortages in Central and Eastern European countries, combined with critical diesel shortages in 13 countries, indicates potential regional supply chain bottlenecks.

Oil prices have created additional pressure, with Brent crude rising from $72.48 to $95.63 per barrel since February's Middle East escalation - a 32% increase that has translated into elevated pump prices. The Netherlands leads with petrol at €2.388 per liter, while price variations across the bloc suggest differing national tax policies and supply arrangements are affecting consumer costs during this period of market stress.
keyPoints: 14 of 27 EU countries hold critical fuel reserves in at least one category, with Romania and Poland showing comprehensive shortages across all fuel types
Jet fuel supplies are under particular strain with 17 countries in critical or warning status, including major economies Germany, France, Italy, and Spain
Diesel shortages affect 13 countries critically, concentrated in Central and Eastern Europe, potentially indicating regional supply bottlenecks
Crude oil prices have surged 32% since February's Middle East escalation, from $72.48 to $95.63 per barrel, driving pump price increases across the bloc
Price disparities are significant, with Netherlands petrol at €2.388/L versus Malta at €1.34/L, reflecting varying national responses to supply pressures
